title: A MoE-LLM-based multisensor flexible fusion fault diagnosis method for rotating machinery

agents.description: A single MoE-LLM-based agent that processes multisensor signals (vibration, acoustic, current) through a multisensor embedding layer, applies sparse Mixture of Experts mechanism within a Transformer LLM backbone, and outputs fault diagnosis results with textual explanations.

genai_model.finetuning_dataset_description: Three multisensor rotating machinery datasets: Intelligent Bearing (IB) dataset, BJTU-A dataset (subset of BJTU train bogie dataset), and Motor Bearing (MB) dataset. These include vibration, acoustic, and current signals with various fault types and severity levels. Fine-tuning uses LoRA with curriculum learning staged training strategy.

orchestration.orchestration_pattern_description: Single-step orchestration where the MoE-LLM processes multisensor input embeddings through a fixed pipeline: multisensor embedding layer → MoE-LLM Transformer blocks → output fault diagnosis response. No iterative loops or multi-agent coordination.

human_integration.human_integration_description: The system is used in an experimental research context. Humans provide labeled sensor data for training and evaluation. No explicit real-time human oversight or interaction modality beyond providing input data and prompts is described.

retrieval.application_domain_description: Fault diagnosis of rotating machinery (bearings, gears, motors) in industrial systems using multisensor data fusion with LLMs for predictive maintenance.
retrieval.problem_description: Existing intelligent fault diagnosis methods for rotating machinery are limited to single-type sensor signals and lack effective modeling of complementary and synergistic information across multiple sensors. LLM-based diagnostic methods are further restricted to uni-signal inputs and rigid input representations that cannot flexibly handle signal absence or heterogeneity, limiting applicability in complex and dynamic industrial environments.
retrieval.proposed_solution: A MoE-LLM-based multisensor flexible fusion fault diagnosis method comprising: (1) a multisensor embedding layer that maps various sensor signal combinations into unified LLM-compatible feature embeddings via CNN feature extractors and connector networks; (2) a sparsely activated Mixture of Experts (MoE) mechanism with uni-signal and fusion-signal expert subnetworks integrated into selected Transformer blocks; and (3) a curriculum learning-based staged training strategy (pre-training extractors, aligning features, fine-tuning experts, LoRA-based task fine-tuning) to enhance transferability across scenarios.
retrieval.research_methodology_description: Controlled experimental comparison of the proposed MoE-LLM method against multiple baseline and state-of-the-art methods on three publicly available multisensor rotating machinery datasets (IB, BJTU-A, MB). Ablation studies evaluate individual components (MoE layers, connector networks, training strategies, text prompts). Hyperparameter studies assess the impact of K (active experts) and LoRA rank r. Domain shift experiments test transferability.

decision_quality_improvement: Proposed method achieves 97.87%/97.85% accuracy/F1 on IB dataset, outperforming best baseline Res-GTLA-Net (97.30%/97.58%) and LLM-based BearLLM (82.29%/82.01%).
On BJTU-A dataset, proposed method achieves 99.76%/99.76% accuracy/F1, outperforming TAR (99.44%/99.44%) and all LLM-based baselines.
On MB dataset, proposed method achieves 99.95%/99.95% accuracy/F1, outperforming TAR (99.89%/99.89%) and LLMFB-2 (98.86%/98.87%).
MoE layers improve average accuracy from 91.56% to 92.24% on IB dataset across signal configurations.
Curriculum learning staged training improves average accuracy from 82.93% (w/o stage 3) to 89.24% (full staged) on IB dataset.
Under domain shift, proposed method achieves 96.87%/96.85% on IB, 97.78%/97.78% on BJTU-A, 99.97%/99.97% on MB, significantly outperforming LLMFB-1, LLMFB-2, and BearLLM.
Text prompts improve average accuracy from 87.74% (no prompt) to 89.80% (with task description prompt) on IB dataset.
Combining vibration and acoustic signals yields 97.87% accuracy on IB vs. 95.78% (vibration only) and 83.08% (acoustic only).
response_time_impact: Inference latency of proposed method is 0.0411 s/sample, comparable to BearLLM (0.0297 s/sample) and LLMFB-1 (0.0500 s/sample).
LoRA fine-tuning keeps training time nearly constant (~11 min per epoch) regardless of rank r from 4 to 32.
Model with pretrained MoE layers converges faster and more stably than without pretraining.
reliability_metrics: Standard deviation of accuracy across runs is consistently low (e.g., ±0.22% on IB, ±0.03% on BJTU-A, ±0.01% on MB for proposed method).
Proposed method requires only 10% of target domain samples for effective fine-tuning under domain shift.

ce_technique_effectiveness.in-context-learning: Text prompts with task description (Prompt 2) improve average accuracy by ~2% over no-prompt baseline on IB dataset; prompts with explicit fault category constraints (Prompt 3) show slightly lower performance than Prompt 2, possibly due to cognitive burden on the small 1.5B model.
ce_technique_effectiveness.chain-of-thought: Curriculum learning staged training progressively increases task complexity, improving average accuracy from 41.06% (end-to-end) to 89.24% (full staged training) on IB dataset.

failure_modes: Misclassification between different severity levels of rolling element faults and normal state on IB dataset due to weak fault signatures of ball defects.
Confusion between motor short circuit and motor shaft bending on BJTU-A dataset.
Confusion among different severities of outer race faults and occasional misidentification of inner race faults as ball faults on MB dataset.
Prompt with explicit fault category constraints causes unstable training on small LLM due to simultaneous fault type and severity classification demands.
limitations: Framework currently limited to vibration, acoustic, and current signals; extending to temperature or image data introduces additional challenges.
Adopted LLM backbone is relatively small (1.5B parameters) and may not fully leverage large-scale pre-trained language models.
Number of expert sub-networks grows exponentially (2^M) with the number of sensor types, limiting scalability.
Model does not explicitly address temporal misalignment or asynchronous sampling across sensors.
End-to-end training without staged curriculum learning leads to poor convergence.

identified_research_gaps: Extending the framework to other signal types such as temperature or image data, which may introduce additional challenges due to heterogeneous feature distributions and distinct sampling characteristics
The adopted LLM backbone is relatively small in scale (1.5B parameters) and may not fully leverage the potential of large-scale pre-trained language models
As the number of sensor signal types increases, the required number of expert sub-networks may grow exponentially, limiting the model's scalability
The model does not explicitly address potential temporal misalignment or asynchronous sampling across signals, which may occur in real-world industrial scenarios
Developing diagnostic frameworks based on larger-scale pre-trained LLMs and incorporating more efficient parameter adaptation methods
Designing more compact and adaptive expert selection and routing mechanisms to enhance the scalability of the MoE structure
Supporting asynchronous or event-driven sensor data to improve applicability in complex real-world environments
